editorialSummary.text: Bustling Italian eatery with varied pizza options from coal-fired to Roman-style, plus beer on tap.

text.text:
Visited Tony’s Pizza Napoletana and had a mixed experience. The garlic bread was very good and definitely one of the highlights of the meal. We also ordered the La Regina pizza. While it wasn’t my favorite choice and I wouldn’t order it again, the pizza was huge and could easily feed 5-6 people.

The outdoor tables along the street created a fun atmosphere, and the servers were friendly and attentive. We waited about 20 minutes, which wasn’t too bad considering how busy the place was.

The biggest downside was parking. Finding a spot in the area was a nightmare, so plan accordingly. Overall, a good experience with great service and atmosphere, even though the pizza choice didn’t quite work for me.

text.text:
Located on the corner of Union and Stockton St.
We have been ordering their pizzas through Gold Belly for years and decided to try the restaurant since we were visiting San Francisco.
Gino was our server and he was welcoming and friendly.
Unfortunately that was where the good ended…
What we ordered:
1.) Garlic Bread $12.00 This was a bit too crunchy over baked but still tasty
2.) Spaghetti and meatballs $27.00 My girlfriend said it was so tasteless and bland, she barely ate it and said she would need to take it home and doctor it up to be edible!
3.) Combo Americans Pizza $38.50 Hubby said his pizza was just ok
4.) Margherita Pizza $30.00
5.) Margherita Pizza with Salame $36.00 unfortunately these two pizzas were with forgotten, never ordered at kitchen or given to other patrons because after a half an hour, I nor our other guest had not received our pizzas. Once we did get them, they were just ok.
The manager did give us dessert, but the food was disappointing.
So bummed, I will continue to order thru Gold Belly, but will not be back to this spot too much hype not enough great tasting food and over priced!

text.text:
Some places earn a visit. Tony’s earned two.

We stopped in during the day for the Margherita pizza, and it was simply perfection—a perfect 10 in our book. The wood-fired crust had the ideal balance of blistered char and airy chew, while the San Marzano tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and basil showcased how incredible simple ingredients can be when they’re done right.

One visit wasn’t enough, so we came back the next evening for the New Yorker topped with sausage, pepperoni, and ricotta. It was a completely different style but just as impressive. The crust stayed crisp beneath the generous toppings, and every bite delivered big New York flavor with Tony’s signature quality.

The atmosphere is part of what makes this place so special. During the day, you can watch the pizzaiolos working the massive wood-fired ovens. At night, the neon lights, packed dining room, lively bar, and crowd gathered outside create an energy that’s impossible to ignore. Even Tony’s by the Slice next door had a steady line of people grabbing slices or whole pies to go.

To round out the experience, I paired the New Yorker with the Diablo Double IPA from Lafayette Brewing, while Beth enjoyed the “That’s How I Roll” bourbon cocktail. Both were excellent choices and complemented the pizzas perfectly.

Yes, there’s usually a wait—but that’s usually a sign you’re in the right place. After visiting twice on the same trip, we’d happily get back in line again.

If you’re visiting San Francisco and only have time for one pizza stop, make it Tony’s Pizza Napoletana. It’s every bit as good as its reputation. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

text.text: Yeah, its that good. Have your pizza anu way you like it. Thin and crispy, deep and chewy etc. They even tell you how to pair the wine. Do yourself a favor and go on a Wednesday afternoon or you're going to be waiting a really long time. Say hi to Gino, he's new and did a great job on his first day!

Do yourself a favor and go on a Wednesday afternoon or you're going to be waiting a really long time. Say hi to Gino, he's new and did a great job on his first day!

text.text: I can see why they’ve won so many awards, lives up to the hype for sure. I gotta go back and try some other pizza styles, they had like 8+ styles to choose from. I will be back to try the New York style and see how it stacks up against one of my favorite New York spots like Lucalis. The chew and char on the crust were the perfect amount. Super light and fresh tasting, you don’t leave feeling sluggish or like you ate too much oil. Great staff too, super friendly and fun people.
